title: festuca valesiaca subsp. hypsophila
intro: Festuca valesiaca subsp. hypsophila, also known as alpine fescue, is a type of grass native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, due to the presence of flavonoids and other compounds. The health benefits of alpine fescue include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as providing antioxidant protection and supporting immune system function. Alpine fescue can be prepared into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is sometimes used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
